title: Chapter Ninety-Six: The Wheel
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"Sword Heaven Group Leader."

The Namgung Family Head spoke without looking at his dead. Namgung Se-jin and Namgung Mi lay behind him -- son and retainer, both cold. He did not spare them a glance.

"I will bear the shame of the wheel."

"...As you command."

The Azure Sky Sword Heaven Group Leader bowed with both hands raised, then turned away. The meaning was plain. *Wheel* -- the old strategy of sending men in rotation against a single opponent, grinding him down through attrition. The Family Head was saying he would accept the dishonor. Risk death to drain whatever remained of the Divine Sword Group Leader's strength.

"Magnificent."

The Divine Sword Group Leader smiled. Not mockery. Something stranger -- genuine appreciation, the way a scholar might admire an elegant proof. Light flashed in the Sword Heaven Group Leader's eyes as he spun back.

"If you want to mock me, do so with your sword."

"No. This is how it should be. In a fight with the Namgung Family Head's life at stake, who could blame you for choosing such a method?"

The bearing of the man they called Desolate Fortress' foremost purple-rank master was unlike anything Yeon-shin had encountered. A strategist's temperament -- detached, assessing, finding satisfaction in the geometry of violence rather than the violence itself.

Their eyes met. The Group Leader's scholar-gentle gaze drew into a crescent.

"Go rest. I don't know how many wrinkles you've given the previous leader, but when you crossed swords with Azure Qilin he looked worried."

"...Then I pray for your martial fortune."

Yeon-shin spoke with clasped hands. The Group Leader's face went blank with surprise.

"To think there's someone wishing me martial fortune. I've lived long to see this." He laughed -- brief, genuine -- and jerked his chin. "Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Genius. Good work."

Yeon-shin conveyed his thanks and turned toward the Desolate Fortress camp. His steps were heavy. It felt like Namgung Se-jin's shadow weighed on him with every stride, pressing down through the soles of his boots. As he left the stage the crowd and the valley and the cold mountain air ceased to exist.

"Hey, hey. Aren't you a bit interesting?"

"Grandmother Evil, have some dignity please."

Several Divine Sword Squad masters including Yue Shou-lin stepped forward, but an old man was already approaching with large strides.

"Yeon-shin!"

It was the first time that voice had called out so intimately.

He raised his head. Ma Yeon-jeok had come within arm's reach. The old man examined him with wrinkled eyes -- checking, searching, the way a man counts fingers on a newborn. Previous Divine Sword Group Leader and Desolate Fortress Elder Council Lord were distant titles. To Yeon-shin, he was just grandfather.

"No major injuries... no internal damage? Very good. Very admirable."

"Yes. Let's talk later."

He said it while already looking past the old man. He could not give Ma Yeon-jeok his full attention -- not when the Divine Sword Group Leader was facing the Azure Sky Sword Heaven Group Leader. Two pillars of opposing forces, the foremost swords of Desolate Fortress and Namgung, squaring off in a martial match rare under the heavens.

*Don't try to follow the movements. I won't be able to. Capture the field of vision instead.*

He raised true qi. Formless flames bloomed from the Hundred Meetings point at his crown. For an instant sky-coloured radiance lay in his eyes -- an upper dantian's flash.

*Divine Sword Group Leader. How strong are you?*

Behind him, Ma Yeon-jeok showed a rare smile. "He said to talk later. To me." Joy seeped from his voice, his face softening into something almost foolish. His grandson who always maintained a blade's edge toward Desolate Fortress' Elder Council -- even this empty promise was enough.

---

Different from the first exchange. The wind blowing from the front simply stopped.

The valley winds had been ceaseless, but not even dust rose in the space where the two masters stood. Energy waves so dense they formed a screen. No outside variables permitted.

*Swoosh.*

Sleeve hems trembled. Sword light flashed.

Namgung Ryeok had trained in the heavy sword -- not the Emperor Sword Form but the Azure Sky Unhindered Sword, driven to its uttermost extreme. A swordsman who could freely blend destructive force into unrestrained paths. The greatest of his generation, they said.

The Divine Sword Group Leader was bare-handed.

He met the heavy sword with empty palms.

**BOOM.**

The valley shook. Tremendous vibrations radiated outward. The hand blade deflected the descending stroke by releasing force that surpassed its power -- clashing with sword force carrying equal ferocity. And through it all the purple scholar's robe did not twitch. Hems quietly settling in rushing storms. Protective energy so dense it formed a shell around his body.

"The sword is quite solid. No ordinary material either. Was it made by the Dwarf Clan?"

He spoke the way a man discusses the weather. A scholar on a walk.

Namgung Ryeok's feet scraped the ground as he was driven back a full *zhang*. Explosive true energy from his bubbling-well point halted his momentum -- and in the instant his body stilled he charged again, stepping the two furrows his own boots had carved.

**SWISH.**

Swift sword now. The precious blade carrying white light split the air in a lightning strike. The main strength of Azure Sky Unhindered Sword lay in its random paths -- complete freedom in the vast sky. Swift sword and heavy sword displayed their might within the same breath.

There was not even the sound of splitting air. The strike rushed to meet the Divine Sword Group Leader's face.

Sharp energy gathered along the Group Leader's hand blade. An invisible sword core.

And at the moment Namgung Ryeok's blade approached within a shoulder's width, the Divine Sword Group Leader moved on a different flow of time.

A single step. Slight twist.

His right arm's hand blade shimmered. Sleeve hems hazily distorted.

**STEP.**

Namgung Ryeok's form in deep blue robes brushed past the Group Leader. His body, which seemed about to fly, sprawled. He could not brake. His balance crumbled as he rolled -- a supreme master's dignity scattering without trace.

Like that.

He stopped moving.

*Instant death.*

The crowd's movements stopped. The commotion of messenger birds and exchanged opinions, the lightness techniques flickering in all directions -- all of it ceased. What even was the purpose of that fight? The sword that the great martial house Namgung wielded at its forefront had crumbled. They had not even taken long.

Namgung Mi. Namgung Se-jin. Namgung Ryeok.

"Sword Heaven Group Leader. You forgot how a junior should fight." The Divine Sword Group Leader adjusted his collar. His purple scholar's robe, dishevelled by the broad hand movements, became neat. Not even blood stains on it. "You should have known how many moves below you were. If you had been careful, you could have lasted a few more seconds."

He spoke in a relaxed voice toward the corpse. Provocation flowing as naturally as breathing.

No one answered.

"It seems you all harbour discontent about the order of seniority. Then -- is the next one, as originally planned, the Elder Council Head?"

"No. This will do."

**STEP.**

The one who answered with his feet was not the Elder Council Head.

Finally.

The Namgung Family Head stepped forward. He wrapped his body in Emperor Sword Form's crushing momentum, his bearing pressing the surroundings like castle walls. He was an Eight Great Families' Family Head, complete as a warrior. Different from his son from the very start.

"Come to think of it..." The Divine Sword Group Leader's head tilted. "Where is the old one? Doesn't act his age."

"Divine Sword Group Leader. Show some dignity."

"Family Head, stop with the useless words. Where did he go? Namgung Elder Council Head."

"I saw Desolate Fortress' Elder Council Head leave his seat long ago. I just matched our numbers with your lot. Do you have the shame to raise objections?"

Yeon-shin recalled what Ma Yeon-jeok had told him. The main fortress' Elder Council Head was pursuing the Seventh Apostle. Days had passed since then.

*How long has it been, still?*

"Isn't that cute," the Group Leader said, his tone grown languid. "Trying to save enough strength to command the main family. Truly excellent judgment. Well -- it can't be helped. I'll deal with the immediate problem first."

Then.

It was the moment the Divine Sword Group Leader raised his right hand. A stream of light arose in his grip and space cracked lengthily.

**CRACK.**

A sword took form -- born from waves of true qi that materialized out of nothing. Seven-coloured light nets lumped together and forged into a single blade, gripped in his right hand. Brilliant, impossible, *alive.*

*It's a different class from my Radiant Sword Style. So that's a light sword.*

"...As flashy as your nature," the Namgung Family Head said. Even before arts close to divine skills, he seemed unshaken. His face was solemn.

"Come."

That was the last word.

**BOOM.**

The two peerless masters disappeared. Only the light trails left by the flashing true qi sword slowly scattered. Yeon-shin could not see the movements that rushed in an instant. Pressure came before sound. The aftermath of their collisions transcended even the realm of explosions.

He had to fight not to be swept away. He raised the Jeong family dynamic technique to its extreme, then released Eternal Blossom Fist paths into the air -- tearing through energy waves that rushed at him like surf.

**BANG. BANG.**

His hands went cold. He did not know how many techniques he threw.

*How much longer--*

One breath felt like an eon.

Then he blinked, and only then did he see them.

The true qi sword in the Divine Sword Group Leader's hand had scattered. But he stood alone. Whole. Looking down.

The Namgung Family Head -- one of the Eight Great Masters acknowledged by the entire murim, a giant of the era -- lay with eyes wide. His upper body had been destroyed completely.

He had not considered family name, blood, or even his children in his view. And he had met his end like his son Namgung Se-jin. Without turning his head, he glared at where the Divine Sword Group Leader stood. The fighting spirit and resentment carved on his face were starkly revealed.

Silence. Terrible silence.

"Well. Collect the corpse."

The Divine Sword Group Leader clicked his tongue and moved toward the Desolate Fortress camp.

"Group Leader, you took quite a few sword hits, didn't you? I counted up to five."

Yue Shou-lin said it casually. Yeon-shin's eyes examined the purple scholar's robe -- now rags. Thousand-silkworm thread, unable to endure the Namgung Family Head's strikes.

"Mm. He was quite strong."

"You're putting up a false front... I can sense your trembling breath. Why don't you compose yourself first with your martial techniques?"

The Group Leader nodded halfheartedly. Yeon So-ha cut in with an innocent face.

"The matter is settled for now. More than that, I'm curious about the reward Group Leader promised Lightning Genius. Murim friendship, was it? Since when did that exist?"

The Divine Sword Group Leader looked back at Yeon-shin. Their eyes met.

The purple-rank peerless master's lips drew an arc, but it was quite an awkward-looking smile.

---
